findings.title: Block primitive has no UI entry point — entire server-enforced safety feature is unreachable
findings.rootCause: The frontend helper `blockUser(userId)` (api/feed.ts:31) is defined but NEVER invoked anywhere in the app — `grep` shows the only importers are api/feed.ts (definition) and Blocks.tsx (which imports only `fetchBlockedUsers` + `unblockUser`). Profile.tsx renders only `FollowButton` for foreign profiles (Profile.tsx:718-725); there is no Block control. Comments in Blocks.tsx:6 and Settings.tsx:347 reference 'a stray Block tap on a profile card' that does not exist in the codebase — the trigger was never built (or was removed) while all the server enforcement (blocks.py, follows.py:104, friends.py:155, feed_events.py block filters, explore block filter) shipped.

findings.title: Explore feed block filter is one-directional — leaks blocked-by users' public-trip cards into the blocker-target's Explore
findings.rootCause: The explore_feed query only excludes trips owned by users the CALLER has blocked: `AND t.user_id NOT IN (SELECT blocked_id FROM blocks WHERE blocker_id = ?)` (feed.py:456-458). It omits the reverse direction (`SELECT blocker_id FROM blocks WHERE blocked_id = ?`). Compare build_feed_context (feed_events.py:1117-1122) and public.py:553-554, which both filter both directions. The inline comment at feed.py:430 even claims 'block-aware Explore' but only implemented one side.

findings.title: Comment count vs. comment list desync after a block (third-party posts)
findings.rootCause: `list_feed_comments` filters out blocked authors: `AND c.user_id NOT IN (SELECT blocked_id FROM blocks WHERE blocker_id = ?)` (feed.py:1135-1145). But `_attach_engagement_counts` computes comment_count with a plain `SELECT event_id, COUNT(*) FROM feed_comments ... GROUP BY event_id` (feed.py:181-186) with NO block filter. The block sweep in blocks.py:148-162 only deletes the blocked user's engagement on the BLOCKER'S OWN feed_posts, never on a third party's post, so B's comment on C's share survives and inflates the count for A.

findings.title: Like count includes blocked users' likes on third-party shares
findings.rootCause: `_attach_engagement_counts` likes query (feed.py:163-168) does `SELECT event_id, COUNT(*) FROM feed_likes ... GROUP BY event_id` with no block filter, and the block sweep (blocks.py:148-162) only removes the blocked user's likes on the blocker's OWN posts, not on third-party posts.

findings.title: Following a blocked user from Friends search shows a false 'Request sent!' success
findings.rootCause: search_friends only excludes users who blocked the CALLER (friends.py:87), not users the caller blocked, so B still appears. `_follow` correctly no-ops on a block edge (friends.py:155-156, returns False), but add_friend ignores the return value and always returns `{status:'success'}` (friends.py:207-209). Friends.tsx:238 then sets searchStatus 'sent' on status==='success'.

findings.title: Follows have no per-account daily cap — open notification-spam fan-out channel
findings.rootCause: The limiter is keyed on get_remote_address (extensions.py:24), and follow_user/add_friend have only the per-IP @limiter.limit('60/minute') with NO `user_daily_count` gate. The per-account daily-cap pattern exists for feed_comment (feed.py:1189, 200/day) and trip_create (trips.py:129, 50/day) but was never applied to follows, which the follows.py docstring itself flags as a bell-spam / 'petty follower-count games' risk.

findings.title: Explore tab is entirely hardcoded English (no i18n)
findings.rootCause: ExploreSection.tsx (lines 49, 61-63, 106, 144-159) and ExploreCard.tsx (line 54) use raw English string literals instead of `t(...)`. The rest of the Feed (Feed.tsx, Friends.tsx) is properly routed through the i18n table, so this tab is an inconsistent gap.

findings.title: Feed bundle lines and several event verbs are hardcoded English (i18n gap)
findings.rootCause: `bundleLine` (render.ts:186-206) returns entirely hardcoded English template strings with no t() call. In `eventLine`, the achievement_unlocked branch (render.ts:326-332) hardcodes 'unlocked' and 'a new badge', and the settled_up branch (render.ts:333-354) hardcodes 'settled up with', 'on', 'a trip', and the 'someone' fallback. Action buttons' title/aria attrs (render.ts:441,463,472,481) are also hardcoded ('Like','Unlike','Comments','Repost to your friends','Bookmark').

findings.title: FollowButton, Blocks tab, and profile Log-Out labels are hardcoded English
findings.rootCause: FollowButton.tsx:85 returns the literal 'Following'/'Follow' (its error toast uses t() but the button label does not). Blocks.tsx:59-63,140,169 use literal English ('Unblock this user?', 'Unblock', 'Unknown user'); the file's own comment notes the relative-time was i18n'd but the modal/labels were missed. Profile.tsx:713 hardcodes 'Log Out'.
